summary: I re-ran the design-coverage check for iteration 2, tracing all 34 business requirements against the current technical design and grounding each check in the discovery notes' facts about what the existing Define UI actually surfaces. I confirmed 32 of 34 requirements are cleanly satisfied by the design's explicit R1–R34 traceability table, with no meaningful gold-plating (only the unused `pause_requested_at` column and unpersisted `user:` params, all trivial and self-acknowledged). I found that both coverage gaps from iteration 1 remain unaddressed and unrebutted, so I wrote a `needs_work` verdict carrying them forward: **F1 (major)** — R19 requires a completed "Repeat from the Beginning" restart to show its fresh, replaced results inline, but the design surfaces only `discovery_notes` and `open_questions`, never the regenerated `business_requirements` (Define's headline deliverable, which has no UI surfacing today), so the person is asked to click Done without seeing the requirements they'd approve; and **F2 (minor)** — "Ask Human" is wired only as an anchor into the `questions.present?` block, so when a paused Define has no current open questions the button is a dead link and R14's free-form-notes path is unreachable in that empty state.
